title: Stylisma patens
taxonomy: Stylisma patens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This family comprises around 1,600 species, including bindweeds and morning glories. Stylisma patens is native to the southeastern United States and is typically found growing in wetland areas. It is a perennial plant that produces small, white flowers. Stylisma patens is often used as an ornamental plant in landscaping and gardening due to its unique appearance and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions.

intro: Stylisma patens, also known as the Slipper Flower or Slipper Gentian, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Stylisma patens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe coughs and colds, as well as to treat skin conditions and digestive issues.
